scope.summary: Commits the C5 fixture: the built session-setup statement list asserted for BOTH profile postures, with two green posture pins (writable takes no read-only backstop; protected keeps one) and a committed-red ordering assertion that trusted setup must precede the backstop on a protected profile.
out_of_scope: Reordering profile_session_statements — A1b owns the fix; this bead owns only the fixture.
Whether the backstop should be re-asserted after trusted setup, or expressed some other way; the fixture asserts only that trusted setup runs first, leaving A1b free to choose the mechanism.
Any change to read_only_setup_statements, SET_TRANSACTION_READ_ONLY, or the trusted-statement validator.

entry_points.name: build_session_context — the profile-to-login-statements path used by every connect
entry_points.trace: c5_a_writable_posture_takes_no_read_only_backstop and c5_a_protected_posture_still_gets_the_read_only_backstop build the real session context from a parsed profile under both postures and assert the presence/absence of the backstop plus the trusted statement

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib connect:: (25 passed, 1 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.
The ordering assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run; it is not cited as proof of anything here.

The fixture asserts the BUILT statement list, not the Oracle outcome. ORA-01456 is the documented consequence of issuing DML inside a read-only transaction and is reasoned from the ordering, not observed — no live database was involved. A live proof needs the D1 harness with a table-backed application context on a protected profile.

Only trusted_session_statements are exercised as the writing case. login_statements and login_script are ALTER SESSION only and are unaffected by an open read-only transaction, so the ordering matters solely for the trusted list.
follow_ups: A1b must delete the #[ignore] on c5_a_protected_posture_runs_trusted_setup_before_the_read_only_backstop, leaving the assertion unchanged, and must keep both posture pins green — it may neither drop the backstop on protected profiles nor introduce one on writable profiles.
